title: Random picks

body.text: Math.random() returns a random decimal between 0 and 1 every time it is called. To turn it into a usable whole number index, multiply by the range you want and round down with Math.floor().

body.desc: A random whole number between 0 and 8

lines: // Pick a random item from an array:

body.label: Why Math.floor?
body.text: Math.random() never returns exactly 1.0, so multiplying by 4 gives a number from 0 to 3.999. Math.floor rounds down to 0, 1, 2, or 3 — exactly the valid indexes of a 4-item array.

exercises.title: Explore random picks
tasks: Run Math.floor(Math.random() * 9) ten times using a loop. Log each result.
Predict: what is the smallest possible result? The largest? Confirm by running many times.
Write a function called pickRandom(array) that returns a random item from any array.
Test it on three different arrays.
exercises.hint: Inside pickRandom: let i = Math.floor(Math.random() * array.length); return array[i];

exercises.title: Pick without duplicates
tasks: Write a function called pickCells(n) that returns n unique random numbers from 0 to 8.
No number should appear twice in the result.
Test by calling pickCells(3) five times and checking for duplicates.
Predict: what happens if you call pickCells(10)? Try it.
exercises.hint: Start with all 9 indexes in an array. Each time you pick one, remove it with splice so it cannot be picked again.

checks.label: pickCells(3) returns an array of length 3

customize: In style.css, change .lit background-color to any color you want for the highlighted cells.
Change the cell size by updating width and height in the .cell rule.
Add border-radius: 50% to .cell to make cells circular instead of square.
Change the gap between cells in the #grid rule.
